data.title: Tokenization makes investing more accessible — Robinhood exec
data.description:
Tokenization could open new opportunities for retail investors to access traditionally restricted asset classes, according to Johann Kerbrat, senior vice president and general manager of Robinhood Crypto, who called it “very important for financial inclusion. ” Speaking at the Consensus 2025 event in Toronto, Kerbrat said that some real-world assets, such as real estate and private equity, are available only to up to 10% of the US population. “You need to be an accredited investor to invest in private equity right now,” he said. “How many people can afford a house or an apartment in New York?” he elaborated
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/tokenization-makes-investing-more-accessible-robinhood-exec?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Judge Torres Shatters SEC-Ripple Pact, Sparking Crypto Regulation Turmoil
data.description:
The SEC has hit a setback after Judge Analisa Torres denied its settlement bid with Ripple, saying the request to void an injunction and cut penalties lacked legal grounds. The ruling delays efforts to dismiss appeals and complicates Chair Paul Atkins’ push for clearer crypto rules.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/judge-rejects-sec-ripple-deal/

data.title: Filecoin, Lockheed Martin send data in space using decentralized data protocol
data.description:
The Filecoin Foundation and Lockheed Martin Space have successfully transmitted data in space using a version of the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) on a satellite orbiting Earth, Marta Belcher, president of the Filecoin Foundation, told Cointelegraph.  Filecoin and Lockheed Martin adapted the system for use in space and successfully tested it, Belcher said during the Consensus 2025 conference in Toronto. The IPFS enhances privacy and security compared to traditional web protocols, such as HTTP, by identifying data based on its content rather than its location. This has additional benefits for data transmission in space, Belcher said
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/filecoin-lockheed-martin-test-decentralized-data-protocol-in-space?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Filecoin and Lockheed Martin Achieve Space Data Transmission with IPFS
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the Filecoin Foundation and Lockheed Martin Space have successfully transmitted data in space using a version of the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) on a satellite orbiting Earth. This achievement was announced by Marta Belcher, president of the Filecoin Foundation, during the Consensus 2025 conference in Toronto. The collaboration between Filecoin and Lockheed Martin involved adapting the IPFS for space use, resulting in a successful test. The IPFS offers enhanced privacy and security compared to traditional web protocols like HTTP by identifying data based on its content rather than its location. This feature is particularly beneficial for space data transmission, as it reduces delays, compensates for data corruption caused by radiation, and enables cryptographic verification to ensure data integrity.

The Filecoin Foundation is a nonprofit organization that governs the decentralized cloud storage protocol Filecoin (FIL), which utilizes the IPFS web protocol for data storage. Lockheed Martin, one of the world's largest aerospace companies, played a crucial role in this space adaptation. Belcher highlighted the advantages of IPFS in space, noting that it addresses the multi-second delay from the Moon and the multi-minute delay from Mars. With IPFS, data can be retrieved from the nearest source, whether it be a device, a nearby satellite, or a lunar station. The distributed architecture of IPFS allows for multiple copies of data to be stored across a global network, reducing reliance on centralized data centers. This redundancy enhances reliability in environments where hardware is susceptible to degradation, ensuring the integrity of sensitive materials such as satellite images.

Interest in decentralized archival storage is increasing among media companies, and the Filecoin Foundation is also exploring potential military applications for this technology. Belcher emphasized the potential benefits for media, noting the ability to maintain a deep archive and access records globally when needed. The FIL token, a utility token within the Filecoin ecosystem, has a total market capitalization of approximately $1.8 billion as of May 16, according to Cointelegraph data. This development marks a significant step forward in the use of decentralized storage solutions in space, offering promising applications for various industries.

data.title: TradFi Stocks Show Bitcoin Exposure Is Not a Silver Bullet
data.description:
TradFi’s relationship with Bitcoin continues to evolve, with 34 public corporations now holding a combined 699,387 BTC—worth over $72 billion. MicroStrategy remains the undisputed leader, holding 555,450 BTC alone. While some view Bitcoin treasury strategies as bullish catalysts, the data tells a more nuanced story: adding BTC to a balance sheet isn’t a guaranteed stock booster. Outliers like Metaplanet have surged over 3,000% since their BTC entry, but many others have seen far more modest gains, or even declines
source: https://beincrypto.com/tradfi-stocks-bitcoin-exposure-silver-bullet/

data.title: Warren Buffett Bitcoin Rejection Deepens: Nubank Stake Fully Liquidated
data.description:
Key Insights: Warren Buffett’s Bitcoin stance hardened further in Q1 2025 after his firm, Berkshire Hathaway, fully exited its position in crypto-exposed fintech Nubank. The move comes as Bitcoin consolidates below its all-time high, and institutional flows into digital assets accelerate.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/05/16/warren-buffett-bitcoin-rejection-deepens-nubank-stake-fully-liquidated/
